page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes a bundled local storage capacity of 1890 GB and is Premium Storage capable.

The underlying x86_64 architecture incorporates advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA extensions. In benchmark testing, the processor demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded operations, achieving 35.44 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.75 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 202.24 GB/sec.

For memory and database workloads, the server delivers an average memory read-only bandwidth of 2357.77 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 160.73 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside 53.73 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. The instance shows strong capabilities in static web serving at 54.89 GB/sec and small-model LLM inference, processing 2367.91 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model.

Given its low-memory configuration relative to the high vCPU count, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. It is well-suited for tasks such as web serving, software compilation, data compression, and lightweight machine learning inference, though workloads requiring large-model text generation or massive database caching may face memory capacity constraints.
description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Configured with 64.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1890 GB local disk, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, delivering top-tier single-threaded compression performance and strong static web serving throughput. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels, the server excels in lightweight LLM inference and data decompression. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient solution for high-compute tasks with moderate memory requirements, making it suitable for web serving, compression pipelines, and localized batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering strong performance for compression and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, integrated local storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 1890 GB of bundled local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong static web serving performance
Optimized for compute-intensive workloads, web serving, and data pipelines
